sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Offensive tradecraft is switching away from using APIs like "CreateRemoteThread", however, this is still largely observed in the wild.
This rule aims to detect suspicious processes (those we would not expect to behave in this way like word.exe or outlook.exe) creating remote threads on other processes.
It is a generalistic rule, but it should have a low FP ratio due to the selected range of processes.
Detects a suspicious svchost process start

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Use Short Name Path in Image
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detect use of the Windows 8.3 short name. Which could be used as a method to avoid Image detection
It is extremely abnormal for svchost.exe to spawn without any CLI arguments and is normally observed when a malicious process spawns the process and injects code into the process memory space.

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Use Short Name Path in Command Line
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detect use of the Windows 8.3 short name. Which could be used as a method to avoid command-line detection
Detects modification of autostart extensibility point (ASEP) in registry.
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Victor Sergeev, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, Gleb Sukhodolskiy, Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community, Tim Shelton, frack113 (split)

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detect suspicious parent processes of well-known Windows processes

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Creation of an Executable by an Executable
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects the creation of an executable by another executable

attributes.magic: PE32 executable for MS Windows (GUI) Intel 80386 32-bit
